content:
Emerging research demonstrates that self-organizing LLM agents outperform traditionally designed multi-agent systems. Instead of preassigning roles, agents autonomously develop effective coordination and specialization.

• Agents given a mission and protocol spontaneously create over 5,000 unique roles across 25,000 tasks  
• Self-organized systems using sequential coordination beat centralized models by 14%  
• Open-source LLMs achieved 95% of closed-source performance at lower cost  
• Predefining roles (e.g., planner, critic) is less effective than allowing roles to emerge organically
